<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the current state and try clicking the red target
# to see if the behavior changes when pieces are touching centers

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("=== Current state ===")
for r in range(16, 45):
    row_str = lines[r][0:32]
    if any(c not in 'cG' for c in row_str):
        print(f"Row {r}: {row_str}")

# Check adjacency between pieces and centers
# Yellow center: rows 20-21, cols 24-25
# Diamond pattern: rows 20-23, cols 20-23
# They are adjacent at cols 23-24

# Green center: rows 38-39, cols 8-9
# Blue bar: rows 34-37, cols 8-11
# They are adjacent at rows 37-38

print("\n=== Checking adjacency ===")
print("Yellow center at rows 20-21, cols 24-25")
print("Diamond pattern at rows 20-23, cols 20-23")
print("They are ADJACENT (cols 23-24)")
print()
print("Green center at rows 38-39, cols 8-9")
print("Blue bar at rows 34-37, cols 8-11")
print("They are ADJACENT (rows 37-38)")

# Now let me try clicking the red target to see if it toggles
print("\n=== Clicking red target ===")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 48}])

lines_after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== After clicking red target ===")
for r in range(16, 45):
    row_str = lines_after[r][0:32]
    if any(c not in 'cG' for c in row_str):
        print(f"Row {r}: {row_str}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
